text.title: Solo Weekly Cadence Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Solo Weekly Cadence Template application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Each weekly run MUST produce exactly two artefacts: a `week-note` (what happened, what was learned) and a `next-week-plan` (≤5 items with explicit cuts). More than two = drift; fewer = no record.
rule.rationale: Solo operator rhythm research — anchors Solo Weekly Cadence Template rule `two-artifacts-only`.

rule.statement: Sunday review MUST read these four inputs in order: KPI dashboard delta, last-week plan items pass/fail, support-inbox themes, backlog top-10. Skipping any input invalidates the run.
rule.rationale: Reviewed against solo-operator field reports — anchors Solo Weekly Cadence Template rule `four-mandatory-inputs`.

rule.statement: The week-note MUST close with a one-line 'next-week-watch' that the following Sunday explicitly checks. No memory hook = each week is independent and the cadence stops compounding.
rule.rationale: Compounding-decision practice — anchors Solo Weekly Cadence Template rule `memory-hook-required`.

rule.statement: Total time-box is 60 minutes: 30 min Sunday review + 30 min Monday prime. Overruns force a cut, not an extension; recurring overruns trigger a scope review.
rule.rationale: Calendar-discipline research — anchors Solo Weekly Cadence Template rule `sixty-minute-timebox`.

rule.statement: Three or more consecutive skipped weeks trigger a clean restart, not a retroactive catch-up. Retro-fill diaries are documentation, not signal.
rule.rationale: Habit-formation literature — anchors Solo Weekly Cadence Template rule `no-catchup-restart`.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
